channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: An 'appalled' New Zealand official says a navy ship didn't sink because its captain was a woman
item.description: New Zealand’s Defense Minister Judith Collins has decried what she called misogynistic online comments about the woman commander of a navy ship which ran aground, caught fire and sank off the coast of Samoa on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A new mayor takes office in southern Mexico after his predecessor was beheaded
item.description: CHILPANCINGO, Mexico (AP) — A new mayor was sworn in Thursday in a city in southern Mexico where his predecessor was killed and beheaded less than a week after taking office.
item.dc:creator: ALEJANDRINO GONZÁLEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy city-hops across Europe, promoting 'victory plan' to allies.
item.description: ROME (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy was city-hopping across Europe on Thursday to promote a “victory plan” that he said “aims to create the right conditions for a just end to the war" against Russia, detailing the proposals to European allies after a scheduled summit involving U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Rome mayor responds after Macron says he'll fight to keep 'Emily in Paris' in France: 'Netflix does not take orders from heads of state'
item.description: "Emily in Paris" is sparking a très dramatique back-and-forth between the French president and the mayor of Rome, who are battling about the Netflix series potentially bidding adieu to the City of Light.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, The Hill

item.title: After a 2-year investigation, South African president won't be charged over $580K hidden in a couch
item.description: Prosecutors say South African President Cyril Ramaphosa won’t face criminal charges over a hidden cash scandal that was revealed more than two years ago and triggered an investigation by a special police unit.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels attack a Liberian-flagged ship in the Red Sea
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Yemen's Houthi rebels attacked a Liberian-flagged chemical tanker on Thursday in the Red Sea, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump rolls out tax breaks, but no specifics, for overseas citizens and auto buyers
item.description: Donald Trump is rolling out more plans for tax breaks without offering details on how they would work and the impact they would have on the federal budget.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: UN-backed experts say Israel is destroying Gaza's health sector and both sides have tortured people
item.description: A U.N. commission has accused Israel of destroying Gaza’s health care system through “relentless and deliberate attacks” in its yearlong war with Hamas.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Krauss, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel continues to weigh options, timing on Iran strike following Biden-Netanyahu call
item.description: President Biden and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu held a call that in part focused on Iran's recent missile attack on the Jewish state, and reportedly how Israel intends to retaliate.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Colombia’s river guardians battle to protect the Atrato amid threats and abandonment
item.description: PAIMADO, Colombia (AP) — Sediment and pebbles are all that’s left on the earth around much of Bernardino Mosquera’s small riverside community in northwest Colombia’s Choco region.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Archaeologists unearth unexpected find inside a tomb likely belonging to a Roman gladiator
item.description: In Turkey, archaeologists unearthed a tomb seemingly belonging to a Roman gladiator of the third century B.C., but were surprised to find the remains of 12 individuals inside.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cambodian fishermen turn to raising eels as Tonle Sap lake runs out of fish
item.description: KAMPONG PHLUK, Cambodia (AP) — Em Phat, 53, studies his eel tanks with the intensity of a man gambling with his livelihood.
item.dc:creator: ANIRUDDHA GHOSAL and ANTON L. DELGADO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China defiant over South China Sea skirmishes in ASEAN talks and blames meddling by foreign forces
item.description: VIENTIANE, Laos (AP) — Southeast Asian leaders stepped up pressure on China to respect international law following clashes in the disputed South China Sea, but Chinese Premier Li Qiang was defiant during annual summit talks on Thursday as he blamed “external forces” for interfering in regional affai
item.dc:creator: EILEEN NG AND JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump-Putin ties face fresh scrutiny with Woodward book
item.description: Less than a month before the U.S. presidential election, former President Trump is facing fresh scrutiny on his relationship with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: What to know about Israel's ground invasion in southern Lebanon
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s ground invasion in Lebanon stretched into its second week, as the Hezbollah militant group fired hundreds of rockets deep into Israel — with no end in sight to the escalating conflict.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and KAREEM CHEHAYEB, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: With roads often blocked by rebels in Congo, boats — the only alternative — have become death traps
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — The overcrowded boat that capsized in eastern Congo last week killed eight members of Serge Nzonga’s family along with 70 others. Days later, he was back on the same route that claimed their lives in yet another boat lacking safety measures.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA and RUTH ALONGA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Han Kang wins the Nobel Prize for literature. She's the first South Korean to do so
item.description: The Nobel Prize in literature has been awarded to the South Korean author Han Kang for what the Nobel committee called her “intense poetic prose.”.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Niemann And Mike Corder, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Turkey's navy starts to evacuate its citizens from Beirut as Israel battles Hezbollah
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Over 2,000 Turkish citizens and some foreign nationals started boarding a Turkish military ship late Wednesday that brought in aid and will take them out of a country being hit by Israeli airstrikes targeting Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: MEHMET GUZEL and ROBERT BADENDIECK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mozambique counts votes in its presidential election as opposition alleges fraud
item.description: MAPUTO, Mozambique (AP) — Mozambique began counting votes late Wednesday in a presidential election that is expected to extend the ruling party's 49 years in power, though the opposition was already alleging fraud and manipulation.
item.dc:creator: CHARLES MANGWIRO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: “We also sent equipment at the beginning of the pandemic,” Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said. “But about the phone calls — it’s not true.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: To cope with 'confrontational hysteria' by U.S. and South Korean forces, North Korea said it will block its border with South Korea permanently and will cut off railways and roads to the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan's new PM dissolves the lower house of parliament to set up a snap election
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — New Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba dissolved the lower house of parliament Wednesday to set up an Oct. 27 snap election, asking people to trust the governing party's policies even as critics said the vote comes far too soon.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Wednesday it will permanently block its border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures to cope with “confrontational hysteria" by South Korean and U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hezbollah steps up rocket fire as Israel sends more troops into Lebanon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hezbollah fired another barrage of rockets into Israel on Tuesday, and the militant group’s acting leader vowed to keep up pressure that has forced tens of thousands of Israelis from their homes near the Lebanese border.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As Israel plots to strike Iran, its choices range from symbolic to severe
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel has promised to retaliate for Iran’s massive missile attack last week. How it does so involves great risk, and could have major repercussions for the archenemies, the Middle East and the world.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu says replacement for slain Hezbollah chief was killed
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Tuesday that his forces have killed the replacement for slain Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah in another blow for the Lebanese militant group.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Hungary’s Orban interrupted, accused of 'selling out' country to Russia, China during EU news conference
item.description: Hungary's Prime Minister Viktor Orban was interrupted at a news conference by an activist and municipal councilor from the opposition Democratic Coalition party.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in Madeleine McCann disappearance case acquitted of unrelated sexual offense charges
item.description: A German court has acquitted Christian Brueckner, who is also under investigation in the disappearance of British toddler Madeleine McCann, in a trial over unrelated sexual offenses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu's Churchill moment comes amid multifront war against Iranian terror, expert says
item.description: Netanyahu is facing his greatest challenge as Israel’s longest-serving Prime Minister while leading the Jewish state in an existential war against the county’s Islamist terrorist enemies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian arms dealer Viktor Bout, who was traded for Brittney Griner, to sell weapons to Iran-backed Houthis
item.description: Russian "Merchant of Death" Viktor Bout has reportedly resumed his role as an arms dealer following his December 2022 release from U.S. custody.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas launches rockets from Gaza one year after Oct. 7 attacks, while IDF strikes terror targets
item.description: Hamas launched five rockets into Israel on the anniversary of the Oct. 7 terrorist attacks that started the war in Gaza, the Israel Defense Forces say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New report finds nearly 200% increase in antisemitic incidents in US since Oct. 7 Hamas terror massacre
item.description: The Anti-Defamation League recorded around 10,000 antisemitic incidents since Oct. 7, marking the largest year-to-year increase since the group started recording such incidents in 1979.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New MH370 project searches for answers on vanished plane
item.description: A new project is searching for answers about what happened to Malaysian Airlines Flight MH370 and its 239 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Boris Johnson alleges Netanyahu may have bugged his bathroom
item.description: Israel also was accused around the same time of planting listening devices in The White House, The Telegraph also reported.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation
